examples.title: Process Creation using fork()
examples.description: Demonstrates creating a child process using fork() system call
examples.explanation: The fork() system call creates a new process. It returns 0 to the child process and the child's PID to the parent. The parent uses wait() to wait for child termination, preventing zombie process.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is process memory size copied

examples.title: Process Scheduling Simulation - FCFS
examples.code:
def fcfs_scheduling(processes):
    """
    FCFS Scheduling Algorithm
    processes: list of tuples (pid, arrival_time, burst_time)
    """
    # Sort by arrival time
    sorted_processes = sorted(processes, key=lambda x: x[1])
    
    n = len(sorted_processes)
    waiting_time = [0] * n
    turnaround_time = [0] * n
    
    # Calculate waiting time
    current_time = 0
    for i in range(n):
        pid, arrival, burst = sorted_processes[i]
        if current_time < arrival:
            current_time = arrival
        waiting_time[i] = current_time - arrival
        current_time += burst
        turnaround_time[i] = waiting_time[i] + burst
    
    # Calculate averages
    avg_waiting = sum(waiting_time) / n
    avg_turnaround = sum(turnaround_time) / n
    
    return waiting_time, turnaround_time, avg_waiting, avg_turnaround

# Example usage
processes = [("P1", 0, 10), ("P2", 1, 5), ("P3", 2, 8)]
wt, tt, avg_wt, avg_tt = fcfs_scheduling(processes)

print("FCFS Scheduling Results:")
for i, (pid, _, _) in enumerate(processes):
    print(f"{pid}: Waiting Time = {wt[i]}, Turnaround Time = {tt[i]}")
print(f"Average Waiting Time: {avg_wt}")
print(f"Average Turnaround Time: {avg_tt}")
examples.explanation: This FCFS simulation sorts processes by arrival time and calculates waiting and turnaround times. It demonstrates how the scheduler selects the first arrived process and runs it to completion before moving to the next.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n log n) due to sorting

examples.title: Thread Creation using Pthreads
examples.description: Demonstrates creating multiple threads using POSIX threads library
examples.explanation: This demonstrates thread creation using POSIX Pthreads. The mutex ensures mutual exclusion in the critical section, preventing race conditions when multiple threads access shared data. pthread_create() creates new threads, pthread_join() waits for thread completion.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of iterations per thread
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) for shared variables plus stack per thread

examples.title: Shared Memory IPC Example
examples.description: Demonstrates inter-process communication using shared memory
examples.explanation: This demonstrates Shared Memory IPC. The writer process creates shared memory and writes data to it. The reader process attaches to the same shared memory segment using the same key and reads the data. This is the fastest IPC method as no data copying is required.
